item.title: It's the eyes, they never lie
item.description:
Milión červených ruží, chceli by sme celý svet.
V objatí sme ako kruhy, bez začiatku, bez konca.
Vetrom nesieš prvé šifry, pot ma šteklí na perách.
Načo lúštiť povedané? Zamotať sa v riešeniach?
Je to všetko tu, leží ti to na očiach.
Nezľakni sa rezných rán.
Pod nimi je skytým písmom vytlačené,
komu dlžíš ďakujem.

Našiel som ho na najvyššom poschodí

item.title: A week of tracking wolves, bears and lynx in the Slovak wilderness
item.description: A week spent reading snow, following predators, and rethinking what comes next.

item.title: Places from my dreams
item.description:
Otvoríš oči a spomenieš si, že si tam, kde si vždy chcel byť.
Kam len dovidíš, ti šeptá pokoj do ucha mäkkú uspávanku.
Zabudni, že aj posledný strom raz padne.
Sny sú ako z cukru, vábia s nekalým úmyslom.
Hladné mraky už zdola šplhajú, nie je šanca ujsť.
Len sa dole cez všetko živé prestrieľať.
Je čas ísť späť, na Zem.

Vietor pohne všetkým, iba Tebou nie

item.title: Volunteering as European Climate Pact Ambassador
The echo of a distorted cry
item.description:
Snívalo sa mi.
Bol som opäť na tých najkrajších miestach, vôňa levandulí mi opantávala každý zmysel.
Počul známe zvuky, videl veci, čo sa nestali. Čas stál a dal sa ľubovoľne pretáčať.
Tu sa dali porušiť všetky zákony, ani Lorentz nemal žiaden dosah.
Platil by som všetkým za návrat.
No minul som to, a ostalo len, čo som zdedil.

A sme opäť sami

item.title: How to predict annual residential energy consumption?
item.description: Predicting annual residential building energy use intensity from constrained open data using machine learning, weather records, and population dynamics embeddings.

item.title: What drives willingness to pay for renewable electricity in Slovakia?
item.description:
As the climate crisis intensifies, shifting electricity generation to renewable sources has become critical. While engineering solutions continue advancing, understanding consumer preferences and pricing strategies is equally vital for accelerating this transition. As part of my bachelor’s thesis at Erasmus University Rotterdam, I investigated what Slovaks are willing to pay for renewable electricity and which factors influence these preferences.

The Challenge

Slovakia faces a unique situation: the country has substantial potential for renewable energy production (24.4 TWh technical potential vs. 30 TWh current consumption), yet only 23% of electricity comes from renewable sources. With recent energy market crises driven by geopolitical instability, understanding consumer willingness to pay (WTP) for renewable electricity became more urgent than ever. The research posed a central question: Can we quantify what Slovaks are willing to pay for renewable electricity, and can we predict these preferences to enable better pricing strategies?

item.title: What premium do green offices charge in London? A causal study
